articles.title: Elon Musk’s worst enemy in court is Elon Musk
articles.description:
Just a simple country CEO who was being tricked by that sophisticated lawyer | Image: Cath Virginia / The Verge, Getty Images	

About five hours into Elon Musk's testimony, I typed the following sentence into my notes: "I have never been more sympathetic to Sam Altman in my life."
Musk's direct testimony was an improvement over yesterday - even if his lawyer kept asking leading questions to cue him in how to answer. But that memory was immediately obliterated by an absolutely miserable cross-examination. For hours, Musk refused to answer yes or no questions with yes or no, occasionally "forgot" things he'd testified to in the morning, and scolded defense lawyer William Savitt. I watched a few jury members glance at each other. During one testy exchange, one woman wa …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Grindr — yes, Grindr — won the WHCD party circuit
articles.description:
General atmosphere during Grindr White House Correspondents' Dinner Weekend Party 2026 at LXIV DC on April 24, 2026 in Washington, DC. | Tasos Katopodis/Getty Images for Grindr Inc.	

Hello and welcome to Regulator, a newsletter for Verge subscribers about technology, politics, and technology learning how to politick. If you're not a subscriber but would like to support our work, please subscribe here. I promise that your money will not go toward paying for a drone-proof ballroom for The Verge staff, no matter how much fun we'd have throwing parties there.
Speaking of parties: The Verge normally wouldn't do a party report from the White House Correspondents' Dinner week, also known as "Nerd Prom," because it's a bit too much Washington insider circle-jerking for normal people to stomach. (This year was weirder than most, …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Splatoon Raiders preorders for the Switch 2 are nearly 20 percent off
articles.description:
Nintendo recently announced a new pricing policy, which knocks $10 off the cost of digital versions of future first-party titles exclusive to the Nintendo Switch 2. Splatoon Raiders, for instance, is available for preorder ahead of its July 23rd release for $49.99 digitally or $59.99 for the physical edition. However, Walmart is the exception in how it’s pricing preorders for the cartridge version. Right now, you can reserve the upcoming for $49.94 — just a few cents cheaper than the digital version. Raiders isn’t a sequel to Splatoon 3, but rather a spinoff that has similar third-person, paint-splatting gameplay set in new locales.
Splatoon Raiders

Where to Buy:

 $59.99 $49.94 at Walmart (physical)
 $59.99 at Amazon (physical)

The same $10 discount is in effect for Yoshi and the Mysterious Book, a side-scrolling adventure with a gorgeous storybook art style that comes out on May 21st. The price of the physical version is $69.99, but Walmart is currently offering it for $59.88, which is similar to the digital edition’s price. Given that prices on many tech and gaming-related products are increasing, it’s nice to see notable discounts on physical Switch 2 games. If you’re looking for other gaming deals, we’ve pulled together the best discounts from Amazon’s weeklong Gaming Week sale.
Yoshi and the Mysterious Book

Where to Buy:

 $69.99 $59.88 at Walmart (physical)
 $69.99 at Amazon (physical)

articles.title: Microsoft reports sinking Xbox revenue as its cloud business climbs
articles.description:
Microsoft's Xbox hardware revenue continues to tumble, with the company revealing a 33 percent decline as part of its earnings report released on Wednesday. Even though the rest of Microsoft's consumer-focused division took a dip, the company's cloud and productivity businesses continue to soar, driving the company toward $82.9 billion in revenue.
Along with declining Xbox hardware revenue, Microsoft reported a 5 percent drop in Xbox content and services as well. Microsoft has seen a significant amount of executive turnover over the past few months, with the retirement of Xbox chief CEO Phil Spencer and the departure of former Xbox presiden …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Kimmel reacts sharply to Trump’s ‘we won’t match that record’ marriage joke during King Charles event
articles.description: During a formal reception welcoming King Charles, Trump reflected on his parents’ long marriage, noting they had been together for 63 years. Turning to first lady Melania Trump, he added: “That’s a record we won’t be able to match, darling, I’m sorry. Just not going to work out that way.”

articles.title: Google Search queries hit an ‘all time high’ last quarter
articles.description:
Google Search queries hit an "all time high" in the first quarter of 2026, according to a statement from CEO Sundar Pichai published as part of Alphabet's earnings on Wednesday.
"Our AI investments and full stack approach are lighting up every part of the business," Pichai says. "Search had a strong quarter with AI experiences driving usage, queries at an all time high, and 19% revenue growth." He also notes that Q1 was "our strongest quarter ever for our consumer AI plans, driven by the Gemini App" and that the company now has more than 350 million paid subscriptions, with "YouTube and Google One being the key drivers."
Alphabet also anno …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Google parent Alphabet's cloud unit beats quarterly revenue estimates on strong AI demand
articles.description: Alphabet's cloud unit revenue surged 63% to $20 billion, exceeding estimates and driven by strong enterprise spending on AI infrastructure. The company's overall revenue also beat expectations, with its cloud backlog nearly doubling. This performance highlights Alphabet's significant gains in the competitive AI market, bolstered by its Gemini models and a key partnership with Apple.

articles.title: US stocks today: Microsoft cloud revenue accelerates as spending growth cools
articles.description: Microsoft's cloud revenue saw a jump in the March quarter. Spending on infrastructure was lower than anticipated. This performance aims to reassure investors about the company's significant artificial intelligence investments. Microsoft is expanding its AI offerings, including partnerships, to maintain its competitive edge in the cloud market. This comes as major tech firms invest heavily in AI infrastructure.

articles.title: Elon Musk’s worst enemy in court is Elon Musk
articles.description:
Just a simple country CEO who was being tricked by that sophisticated lawyer | Image: Cath Virginia / The Verge, Getty Images	

About five hours into Elon Musk's testimony, I typed the following sentence into my notes: "I have never been more sympathetic to Sam Altman in my life."
Musk's direct testimony was an improvement over yesterday - even if his lawyer kept asking leading questions to cue him in how to answer. But that memory was immediately obliterated by an absolutely miserable cross-examination. For hours, Musk refused to answer yes or no questions with yes or no, occasionally "forgot" things he'd testified to in the morning, and scolded defense lawyer William Savitt. I watched a few jury members glance at each other. During one testy exchange, one woman wa …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Meta Shares Fall as Investors Weigh Tech Prospects
articles.description: Meta Platforms Inc. raised its spending outlook for the year, projecting full-year capital expenditures of $125 billion to $145 billion, driven by its AI strategy and higher component pricing. Bloomberg's Sandeep Singh has the latest. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: On the stand, Elon Musk can’t escape his own tweets
articles.description: Elon Musk took the stand for the second day for his attempt to legally dismantle OpenAI.

articles.title: Meta is still burning money on AR/VR
articles.description: Meta is losing billions on Reality Labs each quarter, and its AI expenditures are only going to increase its spending.

topStories.description: The singer is accused of killing 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez.

topStories.title: Colorado retiree learns a life lesson when completing the world's largest puzzle
topStories.description: In retirement, Lou Salas decided to take on a 29-foot puzzle, a map of the globe and its wonders. After over four years, he got to the end. Tony Dokoupil speaks with him about the lesson he's learned.

topStories.title: Young Flyers fans gives Penguins star Evgeni Malkin a perfect Philly welcome before crucial Game 6
topStories.description: Philadelphia Flyers and Pittsburgh Penguins fans bring intense energy to their Stanley Cup Playoffs series, with even young fans getting into the action.

topStories.title: Cowboys lock in George Pickens on $27.3M franchise tag despite failing to reach long-term contract
topStories.description: George Pickens signed his franchise tag with the Dallas Cowboys for the 2026 season, securing a guaranteed $27.3 million after a Pro Bowl 2025 campaign.

topStories.title: WATCH:  Humanoid robots will join baggage handlers at Tokyo's Haneda Airport
topStories.description: Due to industry challenges, Japan Airlines said it will start a two-year trial to see if the humanoid robots can help with baggage handling and cleaning aircrafts.

topStories.title: JD Vance compares Gov Tim Walz to an 'arsonist' for allegedly trying to take credit for FBI fraud raids
topStories.description: JD Vance likens Tim Walz to an arsonist, claiming the Minnesota governor let fraud happen under his watch as FBI raids target Minneapolis businesses.

topStories.title: Illegal immigrant admitted posing as Border Patrol agent in calculated scheme to disrupt deportations: DOJ
topStories.description: A man accused of impersonating a Border Patrol agent to interfere with ICE operations pleaded guilty and faces up to 15 years on firearms charges.

topStories.title: King and Queen lay flowers at 9/11 Memorial in New York
topStories.description: King Charles and Queen Camilla on their state visit to the US visit the memorial in New York.

dailyNews.title: Top US Apartment Owners AvalonBay, EQR to Discuss Combining
dailyNews.description: AvalonBay Communities Inc. and Equity Residential are considering a potential combination that could reshape the US apartment market, people familiar with the matter said.

dailyNews.title: Man allegedly steals fire truck parked outside Colorado jail moments after being released from custody
dailyNews.description: A suspect allegedly stole a fire truck just after being released from a Colorado jail, triggering a pursuit that caused significant vehicle damage.

dailyNews.title: DC shooting prompts alarm about threat posed by attackers crossing state lines
dailyNews.description: Federal officials issued a warning to anyone contemplating traveling to the nation's capital to cause mayhem.

dailyNews.title: What the Royal State Dinner Guest List Says About Trump’s America
dailyNews.description: There were at least 10 American billionaires, six Fox News hosts, assorted presidential pals, no Democratic politicians and not so many British.

dailyNews.title: United flight reports close call with drone just before landing in San Diego
dailyNews.description: A United Airlines flight from San Francisco reported a close call with a drone as it was approaching to land in San Diego, authorities said.

dailyNews.title: Trump EPA chief vows he won’t take ‘morality lessons’ from Dem senator after heated clash
dailyNews.description: EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin and Sen. Sheldon Whitehouse traded barbs over coal plant consumer costs on Wednesday amid Trump's proposed 52% cut to EPA funding for 2027.

dailyNews.title: US singer D4vd bought tools online to dispose of girl's body, prosecutors allege
dailyNews.description: The Tiktok star's attorneys have maintained his innocence and vowed to "vigorously defend" him.

dailyNews.title: Trump says blockade will stay until Iran makes nuclear deal
dailyNews.description: President Trump said he would not drop his blockade of Iran’s ports designed to pressure Iran to make a nuclear deal. Plus, Democrats grilled Pentagon leaders over the war. NBC News’ Ryan Nobles reports.

dailyNews.title: The South braces for more severe weather in the wake of destructive  tornadoes
dailyNews.description: Residents of the Deep South are bracing for another night of severe weather, a day after two tornadoes tore through north Texas.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says US Reviewing Possible Reduction Of Its Troops In Germany
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump said Wednesday that the United States is considering reducing the number of its troops in Germany, amid a row with German Chancellor Friedrich Merz over the Iran war.

dailyNews.title: What the Supreme Court’s Voting Rights Ruling Could Mean for the Midterms
dailyNews.description: Democrats stand to lose at least one blue-leaning district in Louisiana, but the timing was unclear. Florida has approved a redder map, and Republicans in several other states are weighing new districts.

dailyNews.title: Carney ‘strong’ in year one, now must deliver on promises in Canada
dailyNews.description: Canada's prime minister has used Trump's attacks on allies and others to refocus Ottawa's foreign policy.

topStories.title: Grindr — yes, Grindr — won the WHCD party circuit
topStories.description:
General atmosphere during Grindr White House Correspondents' Dinner Weekend Party 2026 at LXIV DC on April 24, 2026 in Washington, DC. | Tasos Katopodis/Getty Images for Grindr Inc.	

Hello and welcome to Regulator, a newsletter for Verge subscribers about technology, politics, and technology learning how to politick. If you're not a subscriber but would like to support our work, please subscribe here. I promise that your money will not go toward paying for a drone-proof ballroom for The Verge staff, no matter how much fun we'd have throwing parties there.
Speaking of parties: The Verge normally wouldn't do a party report from the White House Correspondents' Dinner week, also known as "Nerd Prom," because it's a bit too much Washington insider circle-jerking for normal people to stomach. (This year was weirder than most, …
Read the full story at The Verge.

topStories.title: Trump Says US Has "A Shot" At Crewed Moon Landing Before Presidency Ends
topStories.description: US President Donald Trump on Wednesday said he believes NASA has a good chance of returning astronauts to the Moon's surface before he leaves the White House at the start of 2029.

topStories.title: Trump says Iran ‘choking like a stuffed pig,’ vows naval blockade will remain until nuclear deal
topStories.description: Trump framed the naval blockade at the Strait of Hormuz as a central tool of leverage against Tehran, even suggesting it could be more effective than military strikes.

topStories.description: The man accused of trying to assassinate President Donald Trump took a selfie in his hotel room moments before bursting through security with a pump-action shotgun, prosecutors said Wednesday.

dailyNews.description: Trump framed the naval blockade at the Strait of Hormuz as a central tool of leverage against Tehran, even suggesting it could be more effective than military strikes.

dailyNews.title: Alleged Trump Assassin Took Selfie Moments Before Attack: Prosecutors
dailyNews.description: The man accused of trying to assassinate President Donald Trump took a selfie in his hotel room moments before bursting through security with a pump-action shotgun, prosecutors said Wednesday.

dailyNews.title: Cole Allen case reveals Secret Service failures that could have led to tragedy at D.C. gala
dailyNews.description: New details about alleged gunman Cole Allen's dash into the White House correspondents' dinner while President Trump and other Cabinet officials were in attendance has raised hard questions for the U.S. Secret Service.

dailyNews.title: Secret Service agent was hit by gunfire from suspect at W.H. correspondents’ dinner, sources say
dailyNews.description: Investigators believe that the man charged with the attempted assassination of President Donald Trump shot a Secret Service officer who was wearing a ballistics vest, according to three law enforcement officials familiar with the case

dailyNews.title: Kimmel reacts sharply to Trump’s ‘we won’t match that record’ marriage joke during King Charles event
dailyNews.description: During a formal reception welcoming King Charles, Trump reflected on his parents’ long marriage, noting they had been together for 63 years. Turning to first lady Melania Trump, he added: “That’s a record we won’t be able to match, darling, I’m sorry. Just not going to work out that way.”

dailyNews.title: Comey surrenders over charge of threatening Trump's life in Instagram post
dailyNews.description: Prosecutors say a 2025 seashell photo posted by the former FBI director was a call for violence against Trump.

dailyNews.title: Hegseth clashes with Democrats over Iran war, dismissal of top military leaders
dailyNews.description: The defense secretary sought to rebut lawmakers’ criticism of the Trump administration’s handling of the conflict, which has cost about $25 billion so far.

dailyNews.title: Supreme Court Grapples With Trump’s Plan to Revoke Deportation Protections
dailyNews.description: The case deals with Temporary Protected Status for hundreds of thousands of Haitians and Syrians but could have implications for more than a million from troubled nations.

dailyNews.title: Trump says he told Putin to end Ukraine war, not focus on Iran's uranium enrichment
dailyNews.description: Speaking to reporters in the Oval Office, Trump described the call as productive and said the two leaders discussed multiple global issues, with Ukraine remaining the central focus.

dailyNews.title: ABC can beat Trump FCC's license threat if owner Disney is willing to fight
dailyNews.description: Broadcast license renewals are "all but automatic" due to 1996 change in US law.

dailyNews.title: WATCH:  Trump hosts Artemis II crew at White House after historic lunar flyby mission
dailyNews.description: President Trump welcomed the Artemis II crew to the White House after they completed a record-breaking trip around the moon.

dailyNews.title: Jerome Powell calls his time as Fed chair a 'privilege' during last press conference
dailyNews.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell said his time as head of the central bank has been a "privilege," as he gave his last press conference as chair.  The Senate Banking Committee advanced President Donald Trump’s nominee to succeed Powell, Kevin Warsh. Powell will remain at the Fed after May 15, when his tenure as its chair ends.

dailyNews.title: Brent oil tops $118 after Trump says he will blockade Iran until it agrees to a nuclear deal
dailyNews.description: The latest move higher comes amid reports that the U.S. will look to extend its blockade of Iranian ports.

dailyNews.title: Trump weighs pulling US troops from Germany amid clash with chancellor over Iran war
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump said the U.S. is reviewing a possible troop reduction in Germany amid his ongoing criticism of Chancellor Friedrich Merz over Iran.
